msgs.text:
Turn 13 — lower I9 -base torsor compressed; the missing λ-shadow is already present I continued from the attached history and problem statement only. The original question remains the over-G isomorphism problem for ΓS,ΓH, with F=P⋊G, relators rS,rH, and the required condition πH∘Φ=πS.The prior state was that degree 16 gives a codimension-one high-correction obstruction candidate: the high torsor has rank 35 inside the 36-dimensional quotientL16/im∂15≅L8[2],but Turn 12 warned that this is not yet invariant until lower I9 -base choices in degrees 4,5,6,7 are included.The new point is that the lower-base problem is much smaller and more structured than Turn 12 left it.

Work in the same residual piece:S(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,with target X2=1+A2. This is the residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece used throughout the history.

Starting from the Turn-5 I9 -base liftS(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))≡X2 (modI9),I allowed postmultiplicative lower corrections of initial degrees 4,5,6,7 on all three entries Y0,Y1,q. The first-order ranks agree with Turn 12:d4567 dim(3Ld)18183354 rank(3Ld→Td+1)691830 dimker1291524

Turn 12 already recorded these first-order ranks and noted that only a 210 -subfamily of the 212 degree-4 kernel choices survives the next solvability test.

The new coupled calculation is:D5,6,7:3L5⊕3L6⊕3L7⟶T6⊕T7⊕T8 has rank rankD5,6,7=60.

Since dim(3L5⊕3L6⊕3L7)=18+33+54=105,each solvable degree-4 seed has a 45-dimensional affine continuation fiber in degrees 5,6,7.More importantly, the degree-4 seeds that can be continued all the way to an I9 -base form not a messy nonlinear cloud but a linear 8-dimensional subspace:K4I9⊆ker(3L4→T5),dimK4I9=8.

Equivalently, among the 212 first-order degree-4 kernel choices, exactly28=256 extend through degree 8. So the lower I9 -base family has the shape28 degree-4 seeds, each with a 245continuation fiber at this associated-graded level. Thus the lower-base torsor relevant to Λ16low has 53 effective binary parameters, not an uncontrolled tower.The decisive structural observation is about the Turn-11 missing functional. Turn 11 described the missing degree-16 functional as the parity of the two square coordinates ((AB+BA)4)[2]and(B8)[2]=B16 insideL16/im∂15≅L8[2].

Pull this back one square-level and writeλˉ8 (W)=coeff (AB+BA)4 (W)+coeffB8 (W),W∈L8.

Then the degree-4 survivor space already has rank-oneλˉ8 -square shadow:λˉ8((K4I9)[2])=F2.In concrete terms, a pure degree-4 seed represented by postmultiplying Y0 by Y4 survives the full I9 -base continuation test, and its square shadow contributes the B8 coordinate. Among the 256 degree-4 survivor seeds, exactly 128 haveλˉ8=0 and 128 haveλˉ8=1.

So the Turn-11 disproof route has narrowed sharply:

It is no longer plausible that Λ16low ≡0 for a simple degree reason.The lower-base torsor already meets the unique missing square direction at the degree-8 shadow level. What remains is a secondary-cancellation question: when that degree-4 seed is continued through degrees 5,6,7, and the induced tails in degrees 9,…,15 are killed by high corrections, does the final degree-16 scalar still toggle, or is it always cancelled by the forced continuation? I am not yet claiming that degree 16 dies. But Turn 13 rules out the easiest way to certify the Turn-11 obstruction. The lower-base family is structurally large enough, and aligned enough with λ, that the scalar continuation mapΛ16low:B≤7I9→F2 must now be computed on this compressed 53-parameter torsor, not dismissed.

ESTABLISHED: The lower I9 -base continuation space compresses to an 8-dimensional degree-4 survivor space with 45-dimensional degree-5,6,7 fibers. The degree-4 survivor space has rank-one shadow on the missing Turn-11 λ-square coordinate: half of the 256 degree-4 seeds haveλˉ8=1.

GAP: This does not yet prove ΓS≅GΓH or ΓS≅GΓH. The remaining issue is whether the full scalar continuation Λ16low, after solving the high equations in degrees 9,…,15, actually has image F2 or whether secondary cancellations force it to vanish.

NEXT: Evaluate Λ16low on the Y0↦Y0Y4 survivor seed and one continued 45-fiber representative. If the final scalar is 1, degree 16 dies. If it is 0, test the eight degree-4 survivor generators; that is now the whole structural obstruction test, not an uncontrolled enumeration.[LOWER-I9-TORSOR-COMPRESSED]
